title: Fourth Wing
description: Twenty-year-old Violet Sorrengail is forced into the brutal world of dragon riders, where she must survive deadly trials, forbidden attraction, and war-torn politics while bonding with a dragon against all odds.
themes: war, survival, found family, enemies to lovers, dragons, resilience
tropes: enemies to lovers, slow burn, strong female lead, dragons, academy

title: The Hobbit
description: Bilbo Baggins, a comfort-loving hobbit, is swept into an epic quest to reclaim a treasure guarded by the dragon Smaug, discovering courage he never knew he had.
themes: adventure, courage, home, greed, friendship
tropes: reluctant hero, quest, found family, dragons

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
description: An orphaned boy discovers he is a wizard and begins his education at Hogwarts, where he makes lifelong friends and confronts the dark wizard who killed his parents.
themes: friendship, belonging, good vs evil, coming of age, magic
tropes: chosen one, magic school, found family, mentor

title: The Song of Achilles
description: A lyrical reimagining of the Iliad that follows the tender, tragic love between Patroclus and the legendary warrior Achilles, from boyhood to the shores of Troy.
themes: love, fate, war, grief, mythology, sacrifice
tropes: slow burn, friends to lovers, tragic romance, mythology retelling

title: Project Hail Mary
description: A lone astronaut wakes with amnesia aboard a spacecraft and must piece together how he got there in time to save humanity from extinction, aided by an unexpected friend.
themes: survival, science, friendship, sacrifice, hope
tropes: amnesia, lone survivor, first contact, problem solving

title: The House in the Cerulean Sea
description: A by-the-book caseworker is sent to evaluate a magical orphanage and discovers warmth, belonging, and an unexpected love that changes his carefully ordered life.
themes: found family, acceptance, prejudice, love, belonging
tropes: slow burn, found family, grumpy sunshine, cozy fantasy

title: A Court of Thorns and Roses
description: A huntress is dragged into a faerie realm where she uncovers a curse, court politics, and a slow-burning romance that will test her loyalties and her heart.
themes: love, sacrifice, power, transformation, faerie courts
tropes: enemies to lovers, slow burn, fae, strong female lead, beauty and the beast

title: The Seven Husbands of Evelyn Hugo
description: A reclusive Hollywood icon finally tells the truth about her glamorous and scandalous life, revealing the great love she sacrificed everything to hide.
themes: love, ambition, identity, sacrifice, fame, secrets
tropes: forbidden love, strong female lead, secret romance, frame narrative

title: Mistborn: The Final Empire
description: In a world of ash and mist ruled by an immortal tyrant, a street thief discovers she has rare magical powers and joins a daring crew plotting the impossible: a revolution.
themes: rebellion, trust, power, hope, oppression
tropes: heist crew, chosen one, hard magic system, mentor, strong female lead

title: It Ends with Us
description: A woman building her dream life is forced to confront a cycle of abuse when her past love reappears, in an emotional story about resilience and difficult choices.
themes: resilience, love, abuse, courage, family cycles
tropes: second chance, first love, strong female lead, emotional

title: The Name of the Wind
description: A legendary figure recounts his life story: a gifted, orphaned boy who grows into a notorious magician, musician, and adventurer searching for the truth behind a deadly myth.
themes: knowledge, loss, music, ambition, storytelling
tropes: prodigy, magic school, frame narrative, slow burn

title: Beach Read
description: Two rival writers with opposite styles spend a summer swapping genres and slowly falling for each other, in a witty, heartfelt enemies-to-lovers romance.
themes: grief, healing, creativity, love, second chances
tropes: enemies to lovers, slow burn, forced proximity, banter

title: Dune
description: On the desert planet Arrakis, a young heir becomes entangled in a galactic struggle for the universe's most valuable resource, fulfilling a dangerous prophecy.
themes: power, destiny, ecology, religion, politics
tropes: chosen one, political intrigue, desert world, hero's journey

title: The Midnight Library
description: Between life and death lies a library where each book lets you try a different version of your life, as one woman searches for the existence truly worth living.
themes: regret, hope, mental health, second chances, meaning
tropes: alternate lives, redemption, introspective, magical realism

title: Six of Crows
description: A crew of six dangerous outcasts attempts an impossible heist in a city of magic and crime, where loyalty and betrayal blur and every member hides a secret.
themes: loyalty, redemption, trauma, found family, survival
tropes: heist crew, found family, slow burn, morally grey, multiple POV

title: Pride and Prejudice
description: The spirited Elizabeth Bennet clashes with the proud Mr. Darcy in a sparkling comedy of manners about love, reputation, and overcoming first impressions.
themes: love, class, pride, self-knowledge, family
tropes: enemies to lovers, slow burn, strong female lead, banter

title: The Way of Kings
description: On a storm-ravaged world, a disgraced soldier, a scholarly noblewoman, and an assassin are drawn into an ancient war that will decide the fate of humanity.
themes: honor, leadership, depression, hope, duty
tropes: epic fantasy, multiple POV, hard magic system, redemption

title: Red, White & Royal Blue
description: The First Son of the United States and a British prince turn their public rivalry into a secret romance that could upend an election and two nations.
themes: love, identity, politics, courage, acceptance
tropes: enemies to lovers, forbidden romance, slow burn, banter

title: The Priory of the Orange Tree
description: In a divided world threatened by an ancient dragon, a queen, a mage, and a dragonrider must set aside old enmities to prevent the return of a world-ending evil.
themes: duty, prejudice, legacy, love, unity
tropes: dragons, slow burn, strong female lead, multiple POV, sapphic romance

title: Klara and the Sun
description: An artificial friend with remarkable observational gifts watches the human world from a store window, longing to be chosen, in a tender meditation on love and what it means to be human.
themes: love, humanity, hope, sacrifice, loneliness
tropes: artificial intelligence, unreliable narrator, introspective, literary
